faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Seaford home?
faqs.answer: Signs include small irregular holes in wool clothing, silk items, or upholstery; webbing in closet corners; cream-colored larvae on fabrics; and small buff-colored adult moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice casings that look like grains of rice on clothing or carpet edges. Our Seaford technicians provide thorough inspections to confirm infestations and identify the specific moth species.

faqs.question: What determines the cost of clothes moth control in Seaford?
faqs.answer: Pricing between $550-$5,000 depends on the infestation severity, the number of rooms affected, property size, and treatment methods required. A single-room closet treatment costs less than whole-home fumigation for severe infestations. We provide free inspections for Seaford residents with transparent pricing based on your specific situation and treatment plan.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take in Seaford homes?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 2-4 hours depending on your home's size and infestation extent. Complete elimination usually requires follow-up visits over 4-8 weeks to address all life cycles, as eggs and pupae are resistant to many treatments. Our technicians will create a customized timeline for your Seaford property to ensure complete eradication.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths damage more than just clothing?
faqs.answer: Yes, clothes moths feed on any materials containing keratin, including wool carpets, upholstered furniture, blankets, curtains, taxidermy, and even pet hair accumulations. Many Seaford homeowners discover damage to valuable area rugs, vintage furniture, and stored seasonal items. Our comprehensive inspection identifies all at-risk materials in your home to prevent further costly damage.
